text: The surgeon introduces a V-care manipulator to mobilize the uterus. The round ligament is sectioned at an intermediate point between the lateral pelvic wall and the uterus, marking the first step of the procedure.

The surgeon pulls the ovary upward and lateralizes it to expose the ovarian vessels. The ureter is identified two centimeters below the ovary, ensuring safe coagulation of the ovarian vessels close to the ovary.
The surgeon dissects the posterior leaf of the broad ligament, separating both leaves to expose the uterine artery. This procedure is then mirrored on the opposite side of the pelvis.

The surgeon sections the basic uterine peritoneum by pushing the uterus inward and pulling the anterior peritoneum upward. The peritoneum is incised transversely one centimeter below the basic uterine flexor.

The surgeon performs blunt dissection in the vesicouterine space, carefully separating the tissue layers. This step requires special attention, particularly in patients with previous cesarean sections.
The surgeon coagulates the uterine arteries at the lateralmost point of the uterus, aligning with the cap of the uterine manipulator for precise positioning.
The surgeon coagulates and sections the contralateral uterine artery, with the lumen of the uterine artery visibly observed during the procedure.

The surgeon performs a circumferential colporomy, making an incision around the vaginal cuff. The anterior colporomy is executed one centimeter away from the bladder, preparing the anterior vaginal margin to prevent bladder suturing during closure.
The surgeon performs lateral colporomy, during which minor bleeding is observed. The utero sacral ligament is coagulated and sectioned, ensuring hemostasis.
The surgeon removes the surgical specimen through the vagina. The procedure concludes with the closure of the vaginal cuff using two X-stitches in each vaginal angle with Vicryl-II-O suture material.
The surgeon completes the suture of the vaginal cuff by bringing the X points together at the midline, providing a final view of the pelvis at the end of the hysterectomy procedure.
